name: Tembo Postgres Platform API
what_it_does: Postgres platform with REST API — enabling developers to deploy specialized PostgreSQL instances with pre-configured extension stacks for ML (pgvector, pg_diskann), time-series (TimescaleDB), analytics (pg_analytics, Apache Arrow), full-text search (pg_search/ParadeDB), and message queuing (PGMQ), providing a batteries-included Postgres experience with a control-plane API for instance management.
use_cases: Deploying ML Postgres via Tembo API for pgvector + pg_diskann vector search without separate infrastructure
Creating analytics-optimized Postgres via Tembo for columnar storage with pg_analytics + DuckDB
Managing Postgres instances via Tembo API for cloud-hosted Postgres with extension stack management
Using PGMQ via Tembo for PostgreSQL-native message queuing without separate broker infrastructure
Scaling Postgres configurations via Tembo API for compute and storage adjustment of deployed instances
not_for: Extremely high-scale OLTP requiring distributed transactions (use CockroachDB or YugabyteDB)
Teams needing multi-region active-active replication built-in
best_when: A developer wants a managed Postgres deployment with specialized extension stacks (ML/vector, time-series, analytics, search, queuing) pre-configured — getting pgvector + TimescaleDB + pg_analytics without manual extension setup.
avoid_when: You need a standard Postgres instance without specialized extensions, or need enterprise multi-region HA that managed RDS/Aurora provides.

auth.notes: API key (JWT token) via Authorization: Bearer header. Token from Tembo Cloud dashboard. Control plane API at api.tembo.io/v1/. Organization ID and instance ID required for instance operations. Postgres connection: standard PostgreSQL connection string to provisioned instance. Tembo Cloud: multi-cloud managed platform (AWS). Open-source: Tembo Operator available for self-hosted Kubernetes deployment. Stack types: 'stacks' define pre-configured extension bundles (ml-gpu, olap, vector, timeseries, etc.).

pricing.paid_tiers: Usage-based compute + storage; General Purpose from $0.028/hour; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: US company (Austin, TX). Founded 2023 by Samay Sharma and friends (ex-Citus/Microsoft veterans). $18M seed funding (Benchmark, Andreessen Horowitz). Extension focus: 150+ Postgres extensions available via trunk (Tembo's extension registry). Stacks: pre-configured extension bundles — VectorDB, OLAP, TimeSeries, MessageQueue, Standard, ML, DataWarehouse stacks. PGMQ: Postgres Message Queue — open-source extension for queue-like patterns. trunk: extension registry for Postgres — like npm for Postgres extensions. Tembo Operator: open-source Kubernetes operator for self-hosted Tembo stacks. Open-source first: core technology open-sourced, cloud is managed offering.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits per organization; Postgres connections per instance plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Bearer JWT token. Org ID + Instance ID required. Stacks define extension bundles. AWS-only currently. US company Austin. SOC2. Free hobby tier. Open-source Tembo Operator. trunk extension registry.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Instance creation: idempotent by instance name within org. Stack configuration: PUT operations are idempotent. Extension management: idempotent by extension name. Instance reads fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Organization ID + Instance ID: both required for instance-level API operations — retrieve from dashboard
Stack selection: choose stack type at creation (VectorDB, OLAP, TimeSeries, etc.) — changing stack type requires new instance
JWT token: Tembo uses JWT not static API key — tokens may expire; implement refresh logic
Postgres connection: use standard Postgres client to connect to Tembo instance, separate from control plane API
Extension availability: not all 150+ extensions available on all stacks — verify extension compatibility with chosen stack
AWS-only currently: Tembo Cloud is US/AWS-based — self-hosted Tembo Operator for other clouds

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. JWT Bearer token. SOC2 certified. Postgres SSL required for connections. Org and instance isolation. Extensions: open-source extensions — review each extension's security model. PGMQ: message content stored in Postgres — follows Postgres security model. Open-source Tembo Operator: auditable for self-hosted deployments. trunk: extension registry — verify extension provenance before installing.
